title: The Midnight Reflection in Ashford Hall
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned antiquarian, was found dead in the drawing-room with a gilt mirror, mantel clock, solitary candle and a family photograph. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Victor Hargreaves
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Dr. Malcolm Pike helped me shift the candle to light the victim's papers. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Clara Beaumont and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Dr. Malcolm Pike and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Clara Beaumont
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the clock and did not go near the mirror.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Dr. Malcolm Pike
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, while Victor Hargreaves shifted the candle to light the victim's papers, I moved the mirror aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Victor Hargreaves and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Clock
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the clock. Eleanor Whitcombe has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Outline beneath Candle
clues.detail: The clean outline beneath the shifted base of the candle is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Victor Hargreaves and Dr. Malcolm Pike both state that they shifted the candle to light the victim's papers at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Clock
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split on the clock. Clara Beaumont's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Position of Mirror
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the mirror is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack. Dr. Malcolm Pike states that they moved the mirror aside at 8:45 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Mark on Photograph
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the photograph. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Clara Beaumont killed Eleanor Whitcombe because Clara had discovered that Eleanor was about to expose her embezzlement of the family trust.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was killed by a heavy blow from the clock.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Clara Beaumont claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The physical trace on the candle corroborates the action described by Victor Hargreaves, while the trace on the mirror corroborates the action described by Dr. Malcolm Pike. They left together at 8:45 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, fine copper-coloured silk threads on the clock directly contradict Clara Beaumont's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
